When contacting business to business always use a phone call followed by an email and when you send an email out I want you to actually intercept it with a phone call. Let me explain this. Look the most effective form of contact follow-up for business to business is phone call followed by email. I took a phone call I followed it with an email. The truth is it's not the scenario that's so important. It's the follow-up. It's the combination of activities not depending upon one email or one phone call. So when I take a phone call or make a phone call make an introduction out or take one in I want to follow it up with email. Flip that if I send an email out to a prospect or client I want to intercept that email out with a phone call. Hey John Grant Cardone I just sent you an email. I don't know who you are. You're gonna be getting an email. John told me you were interested in this product. I thought I'd send your proposal on it. This is where I work. This is what I do. Look at the email. I'll call you back tomorrow. Phone calls followed by email is the most effective form of following business to business and I'm gonna also tell you this. When you're business to a customer phone calls followed by emails are also very effective. Use them both ways.
